Documentation or examples showing how to construct OData URLs (e.g., using $top and $skip) corroborate the method to retrieve data incrementally, which is consistent with the need to perform live API calls to determine exact counts or extents. Mentions that some clients require API tokens and how tokens can be supplied as URL parameters speak to token-based access, which is relevant to assessing whether the Richmond Legistar instance is publicly accessible without a token. References to OData URL conventions and filtering illustrate the mechanism to access data rather than confirm a fully token-free public endpoint. There are also references to specific endpoints and examples, which are supportive of understanding the available API surface, though they do not resolve the exact counts or age range without live queries. Finally, some results indicate error conditions such as resource-not-found messages, which can imply API availability issues or endpoint-specific problems, but these are not definitive about the general state of accessibility or SLA. Taken together, these excerpts support the high-level observation that certain operational details (totals, oldest record dates, SLA for GeoHub, and precise CORS/rate-limit policies) are not definitively documented in the provided material, while they do provide practical guidance on how to query and paginate data and on token-based access expectations.

excerpts: Query replies are limited to 1000 responses. Despite this limit, some calls can still return large amounts of data.
GET requests return only the items marked as public and visible on InSite.
Use paging and filtering to limit queries. Following this approach reduces server load and speeds up responses.

To view all agenda matters using the ODATA parameters by limiting smaller set of items, use the following endpoint:
https://webapi.legistar.com/v1/{ Client}/matters?$top=10&$skip=0

To view all agenda matters using the ODATA parameters by limiting larger set of items, use the following endpoint:
https://webapi.legistar.com/v1/{ Client}/matters?$top=10&$skip=10
Some clients require API tokens for access. If you receive an unauthorised response when you make read-only requests, refer to your token policy.

If you have a token, add it as a URL parameter to your API request.
For example: https://webapi.legistar.com/v1/{ Client}/matters?token=verylongbase64token

```
https://webapi.legistar.com/v1/{Client}/matters
```

executive_summary.description: A high-level summary of the key findings regarding Richmond, Virginia's Legistar, GeoHub, and EnerGov data systems, directly addressing the user's main questions.

client_slug.description: The confirmed API client slug for Richmond's Legistar instance.

is_publicly_accessible.description: Confirms if the API is publicly accessible without special permissions.

requires_token.description: Indicates whether an authentication token is required for API access.

odata_api_url.description: The full OData API URL for accessing the Legistar data.

total_matters_count.description: The total number of legislative matters available in the Legistar system.

oldest_record_date.description: The date of the oldest legislative record accessible via the API.

cors_restrictions_enforced.description: Indicates if Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) restrictions are enforced for browser-based fetches.

rate_limit_details.description: Information regarding the effective rate limits for the Legistar API.

update_cadence.description: The actual update frequency of the GeoHub Development Tracker layer.

documented_sla.description: Details of any documented Service Level Agreement (SLA) for the layer's data freshness.

last_updated_date.description: The most recent date the data layer was updated.

layer_name.description: The official name of the ArcGIS Feature Service layer.

category.description: The planning category the layer belongs to, e.g., 'Special Use Permits', 'Plans of Development', 'Board of Zoning Appeals'.

rest_endpoint_url.description: The full REST endpoint URL for the feature service layer.

source_url.description: The URL of the source page on the Richmond GeoHub or ArcGIS portal providing information about the layer.

arcgis_planning_and_zoning_layers.description: A list of ArcGIS Feature Service layers that Richmond, VA publishes for planning, zoning, and development. Each item should detail the layer name and its corresponding REST endpoint, specifically for Special Use Permits, Plans of Development, and Board of Zoning Appeals.

has_public_api_or_export.description: Indicates whether the EnerGov permit system exposes any public API or data export capability.

access_method_details.description: A description of the available data access methods, if any.

public_portal_url.description: The URL for the public-facing EnerGov Online Permit Portal.

api_documentation_url.description: A link to any available API documentation for the EnerGov system.

unconfirmed_information_summary.description: A summary of any questions that could not be definitively answered based on the provided research, such as precise rate limits, the exact total count of legislative matters, or documented SLAs.
